Avetta Argentina Annual Update

In Argentina the limitation of the working hours is a daily and week limitation. An employee can work up to 8 hours a day and up to 48 hours a week. Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and seasonal workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment. Do not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.

Any unexpected injury or incident that occurs at work or commuting to and from work is classified as a work accident. Occupational diseases/illnesses are those incidents that occur due to the scope of the work being carried out or due to the hazards present within the work environment.

Temporary Work disability occurs when a worker has an accident, including while in transit, or suffers from an occupational disease, and the injury suffered prevents him from carrying out his usual tasks. This incapacity begins the day after the first manifestation of disability and ends when the worker is discharged from the hospital, or is declared to have Permanent Work Incapacity, if two years have passed since the first manifestation of disability, or if the injured party dies.
Please enter the number of temporary occupational disability incidents for the given year.

Please indicate the number of days lost from work for all temporary occupational disability incidents. The number of days inputted should exclude the day the incident occurred and the day the employee was able to return to normal work for all incidents.
Please enter the number of days lost from work for the given year for temporary occupational disability incidents where the employee was unable to work.

Please indicate the number of days for all temporary occupational disability incidents where the employee continued working but was unable to follow their ordinary occupation so instead performed alternate or light duty; and/or put on restricted work/time. The number of days inputted should exclude the day the incident occurred and the day the employee was able to return to normal work for all incidents.
Please enter the total number of days which were considered restricted work for temporary occupational disability incidents where the employee continued working, for the given year.

Those incidents that resulted in a permanent reduction (loss of limb, loss of senses, etc.) in the employee's working capacity but the decline is less than 66% of the worker's physical integrity and ability to work.
Please enter the number of partial permanent occupational disability incidents for the given year.

Those incidents that resulted in a permanent reduction (loss of limb, loss of senses, etc.) in the employee's working capacity and the decline is equal to or greater than 66% of the worker's physical integrity and ability to work.
Please enter the number of total permanent occupational disability incidents for the given year.

This is defined as a disability where the employee requires continual assistance from another person to perform basic and necessary activities to live a normal life on a daily basis. This is the most severe form of employee disability and is usually reserved for those incidents where the employee will require assistance from another person for the remainder of their life.
Please enter the number of great disability incidents for the given year.
An injury or incident is considered a reentry or reaggravation incident if it can be attributed or is similar to a previously reported and identified work accident.
Please enter the number of reentry / reaggravation incidents for the given year.
Those incidents which were not classified above but medical treatment was administered to the injured individual. Incidents where only first aid was administered should not be counted.
Please enter the number of other recordable incidents for the given year.

Guidance: Calculation and Definition of Incident Index -The Incident Index reflects the number of notified cases (due to work accidents, occupational diseases, commuting accidents and readmissions) with at least one day off work for every thousand covered workers (includes cases with disabling sequelae, with or without sick leave). The index is calculated for a period of one year and corresponds to a specific geographical area (country or province). -IIG = (Cases reported with sick leave and cases with disabling sequelae with or without sick leave / Covered Workers) * 1000 \n\n Please enter 0 if your company had no incidents
Please enter your Company’s Incident Index for the given year.

Guidance: Calculation of Severity Index -The Severity Index reflects the number of days not worked in the year, for every thousand workers covered -ISG = (Days not worked / Workers Covered) *1000 \nPlease enter 0 if your company had no incidents
Please enter your Company’s Severity Index for the given year.

Guidance: Calculation of Frequency Index -Number of cases reported with days off work or disabling sequelae, every million hours worked -IFG = (Cases reported with sick leave and cases with disabling sequelae with or without sick leave / Men Hours Worked) * 1,000,000 \n\nPlease enter 0 if your company had no incidents
Please enter your Company’s Frequency Index for the given year.

Guidance: Calculation of Fatality Index -It is calculated as the number of workers who died due to work accidents, occupational diseases, commuting accidents or readmissions, every one hundred thousand notified cases. The index is calculated for a period of one year and corresponds to a specific geographical area (country or province) -ILG - (Deceased Workers / Notified Cases) * 100,000 \n\n Please enter 0 if your company had no fatalities
Please enter your Company’s Fatality Index for the given year.

The Superintendencia de Riesgos del Trabajo (SRT) and the Aseguradoras de Riesgos del Trabajo (ART) are responsible for monitoring, documenting, and reporting employer compliance with statutory programs for high accident rate employers, such as PESE and PESE-PYMES. SRT issues formal resolutions (such as Resolution 20/2018 for PESE-PYMES) that include lists of employers designated under these programs. Companies can access public resolutions and lists through the SRT’s official website or by contacting the SRT directly. ART provides a formal notification directly to employers of their inclusion in PESE or PESE-PYMES.
If yes, please upload supporting documents from ART or SRT.
